---
title: "Minimum Wage and Teen Employment"
subtitle: "A difference-in-differences example"
format: html
---

## Question

Did the January 2019 minimum-wage increase reduce the teen
employment rate in treated counties? We estimate a two-way
fixed-effects difference-in-differences model on a simulated
county-month panel.

## Specification

$$
\text{emp}_{it} = \beta\,(\text{treat}_i \times \text{post}_t)
  + \alpha_i + \gamma_t + \varepsilon_{it}
$$

with county fixed effects $\alpha_i$, month fixed effects
$\gamma_t$, and standard errors clustered by county.

## Result

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
print(Path("output/tables/did_main.txt").read_text())
```

The estimated effect is a fall of about 1.9 percentage points
in the teen employment rate, consistent with the value built
into the simulation.

![Treated vs control means](output/figures/event_means.png)
